posts.title: A script for CentOS 6 DNS Server
posts.text: This is a script used for building DNS Server(Contains only forward parsing) based on CentOS 6. All of contents are original, please respect the intellectual property, thank you. DNS ServerServer Script Source Codeyou should execute this script as ‘root’ user 1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253if [ `id -u` -eq 0 ];thenecho "-------------installing bind-------------"yum -y install bindecho "-------------installing bind completed-------------"NAMED1=/etc/named.confNAMED2=/etc/named.rfc1912.zonesNAMED3=/var/namedread -p "-------------please input domain：" DOMAINread -p "-------------please input IP：" IPZONE=$DOMAIN.zoneecho "-------------configuring name.conf-------------"sed -i -e '/listen-on/s/127.0.0.1/any/' $NAMED1sed -i -e '/allow-query/s/localhost/any/' $NAMED1echo "-------------configuring named.rfc1912.zones-------------"sed -i -e '/'$DOMAIN'/,+3d' $NAMED2cat >> $NAMED2 << EOFzone "$DOMAIN" IN { type master; file "$DOMAIN.zone";};EOFecho "-------------configuring $ZONE-------------"cp -p $NAMED3/named.localhost $NAMED3/$ZONEsed -i -e 's/@/'$DOMAIN'./2' $NAMED3/$ZONEsed -i -e '/NS/s/@/'$DOMAIN'./' $NAMED3/$ZONEsed -i -e 's/127.0.0.1/'$IP'/1' $NAMED3/$ZONEcat >> $NAMED3/$ZONE << EOFwww IN A $IPEOFcp -p $NAMED3/$ZONE $NAMED3/chroot$NAMED3/$ZONEecho "-------------launching named server-------------"service named restartsed -i '/^nameserver.*/d' /etc/resolv.confcat >> /etc/resolv.conf << EOFnameserver 127.0.0.1EOFecho "-------------configuring DNS server completed-------------"else echo "-------------please use 'root' use to execute-------------"fised -i -e '/power.com/,+3d' /etc/named.rfc1912.zones after executing script, you should set firewall to allow DNS passing DNS Clientyou should execute this script as ‘root’ user Client Script Source Code1234567891011121314if [ `id -u` -eq 0 ];thenread -p "-------------please input DNS server's IP：" IPsed -i '/^nameserver.*/d' /etc/resolv.confcat >> /etc/resolv.conf << EOFnameserver $IPEOFecho "-------------configuring DNS client completed-------------"else echo "-------------please use 'root' use to launch!-------------"fi

posts.title: Build a Samba Server on CentOS 6
posts.text: This article introduces how to build a samba server on CentOS 6 and some problems that you may met. All of contents are original, please respect the intellectual property, thank you. Configure Samba Server1. Install Sambause yum to install samba as ‘root’ user 1yum -y install samba 2. Create A Samba Usercreate a user 12useradd smbuser #'smbuser' can be replaced by any legal name that you wantpasswd smbuser #then you can input legal password and repeat it, here uses 'smbuser' as password add this user to samba server 1smbpasswd -a smbuser #'smbuser' should be taken place of the user that you just created 3. Create Share Foldercreate a folder that you will share and set authorization 123cd /home/smbuser #enter smbuser catalogmkdir share #create 'share' folderchmod 777 share #grant folder autority 4. Edit Configurationedit samba configuration 1vim /etc/samba/smb.conf add a new item as following 1234[myshare] path = /home/smbuser/share writable = yes guest ok = no the added location is shown as following figure 5. Test Configurationtest whether configuration is right 1testparm if system shows ‘Loaded services file OK’, it indicates that configuration of samba server is exactly correct. Otherwise, it will shows error information. 6. Start Serverstart the service 1service smb start Test Server1. Get Local IPget current local IP 1ifconfig my ip is 192.168.232.128 as following figure 2. Install Samba Clientinstall samba client 1yum -y install samba-client 3. Test Samba Clienttest samba client, here ‘192.168.232.128’ fills your ip as above, ‘smbuser’ fills samba user you just created 1smbclient -L \\192.168.232.128 -U smbuser if you see the content as below, after input your password of ‘smbuser’, you have configured samba server successfully FAQQ1. I Can’t Connect The Samba Server By Other CentOS?when try to connect the server, system shows “Connection to ‘ip’ failed (Error NT_STATUS_HOST_UNREACHABLE)” as figure solution for Q1try to allow samba service to be accessible from firewall Q2. I Can’t Enter My Share Folder On Windows 10?when try to enter the share folder, system shows “Windows cannot access \\‘your accessing route’. You do not have permission to access \\‘your accessing route’. Contact your network administrator to request access.” solution for Q2this is about folder security settings, try to use command as following 1chcon -R -t samba_share_t /home/smbuser/share #'/home/smbuser/share' fills path of your share folder

posts.title: Build a DHCP Server on CentOS 6
posts.text: This article introduces how to build a samba server on CentOS 6. All of contents are original, please respect the intellectual property, thank you. Configure DHCP Server1. Install DHCPuse yum to install samba as ‘root’ user 1yum -y install dhcp 2. Get Local IPget current local IP 1ifconfig my ip is 192.168.232.128 as following figure 3. Edit Configurationedit dhcpd.conf file to set configuration 1vim /etc/dhcp/dhcpd.conf add a new item as following 123456789101112ddns-update-style interim; ignore client-updates; subnet 192.168.232.0 netmask 255.255.255.0 { #'192.168.232.0' should be replaced by your IP network segment range 192.168.232.200 192.168.232.210; #custom option domain-name-servers ns.example.org; option domain-name "example.org"; option routers 192.168.232.128; #replaced by your IP option subnet-mask 255.255.255.0; default-lease-time 600; #custom max-lease-time 7200; #custom} the added location is shown as following figure 4. Change Permissionedit dhcpd file to change permission 1vim /etc/rc.d/init.d/dhcpd 123456789changeuser=dhcpdgroup=dhcpdtouser=rootgroup=root 5. Start Serverstart dhcp service 1service dhcpd start Test Server1. Enable DHCPenable dhcp in network configuration 1setup => network configuration => device configuration => “your network card name” ethernet => check “use dhcp” with star then “ok” and “save and quit” 2. Check Local IPcheck current local IP 1ifconfig if system shows IP belongs to the range that you set like following figure, you have configured DHCP server successfully

posts.title: Build a FTP Server on CentOS 6
posts.text: This is a script used for building FTP Server based on CentOS 6. All of contents are original, please respect the intellectual property, thank you. FTP ServerServer Script Source Codeyou should execute this script as ‘root’ user 1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132if [ `id -u` -eq 0 ];thenecho "-------------installing vsftpd-------------"yum -y install vsftpdecho "-------------installing vsftpd completed-------------"VSFTPD1=/etc/vsftpd/vsftpd.confVSFTPD2=/etc/vsftpd/chroot_listread -p "-------------please input ftp user：" FTPUSERecho "-------------configuring vsftpd.conf-------------"sed -i -e '/#chroot_list_enable/s/#//' $VSFTPD1sed -i -e '/#chroot_list_file/s/#//' $VSFTPD1echo "-------------add ftp user-------------"useradd -d /home/$FTPUSER -g ftp -s /sbin/nologin $FTPUSERpasswd $FTPUSERsed -i '/.*/d' $VSFTPD2cat >> $VSFTPD2 << EOF$FTPUSEREOFecho "-------------close selinux-------------"setsebool -P ftp_home_dir 1service vsftpd restartecho "-------------configuring FTP server completed-------------"else echo "-------------please use 'root' use to execute-------------"fi after executing script, you should set firewall to allow FTP passing FTP Client On Windows 10use “cmd” to enter command shell, then input “ftp 192.168.232.128”(here ip should be replaced by yours that can be seen by command “ifconfig” on CentOS 6)after input ftp user name and password, you have enter ftp client successfully
